title: IPv6 and Modern Programmable Networks · Protocol Recall
summary: Extend the branch design for IPv6, centralized policy, overlays, zero trust, SASE, and infrastructure as code. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a dual-stack and programmable-network transition plan after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compress, expand, classify, and subnet IPv6 addresses and prefixes without inventing IPv4 broadcast behavior.
Distinguish global unicast, link-local, unique local, multicast, anycast, loopback, and unspecified IPv6 addresses.
Explain SLAAC, router advertisements, DHCPv6 choices, neighbor discovery, and default-gateway learning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.
